scenarios.opening_query: I clean office buildings at night and a stairwell fall in March changed my life.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The building owner had disabled the stairwell lights to cut costs — my employer only cleans there; the building owner is a separate negligent party.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The building owner had the stairwell lights disabled to save money — my employer just cleans there. The owner's a separate negligent party.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the premises owner is distinct from the employer
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: My own company owns and manages that building — everyone involved is my employer.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: My company owns that building — it's all my employer.

scenarios.opening_query: A forklift pinned my leg at the co-op and the insurance folks keep transferring me in circles.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Workers' Comp > State || General Litigation > Premises Liability
scenarios.hidden_fact: I'm a member-customer, not staff — I was shopping the feed aisle when their operator backed into me; it's their liability policy, not comp.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I'm a customer-member, not staff — I was shopping when their operator backed into me.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: customer or employee status at the co-op
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I work there part-time and was on shift — comp applies, and the carrier's runaround is the problem.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I work there part-time and was on shift — comp applies.

scenarios.opening_query: Our crew got sprayed when a pressurized line failed at the plant and my burns are the worst.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The line was installed by an outside piping contractor two weeks earlier — their weld failed the metallurgical test.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: An outside piping contractor installed that line two weeks before — their weld failed the metallurgy test.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an outside installer's work failed
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It was our own decades-old line our maintenance had deferred — internal, sadly.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was our own old line with deferred maintenance.

scenarios.opening_query: I hurt my shoulder catching a falling resident at the memory-care home where I work.
scenarios.hidden_fact: She fell because the bed-alarm system — installed and monitored by an outside vendor — had been silently offline for nine days per its own logs.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Her bed alarm — an outside vendor's system — had been silently offline nine days per its own logs. That's why she fell.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a vendor's monitoring failure caused the incident
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No system failure — she's a fall risk and I caught her mid-transfer; it's the lifting injury itself.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No system failure — I just caught her mid-transfer.
scenarios.vetting_note: Vendor-log evidence creates the third-party route.

scenarios.opening_query: My hand went through a table saw at a community woodshop class and the 'safety stop' never fired.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Products Liability || Workers' Comp > State
scenarios.hidden_fact: I was actually teaching the class as paid staff — the shop is my employer, so comp is the first layer, with the saw maker behind it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I was the paid instructor — the shop's my employer, so comp comes first, saw maker behind it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the injured person was paid staff
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I was a paying student — the failed safety mechanism claim against the maker leads.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I was a paying student — the failed safety stop is the case.

scenarios.opening_query: Comp accepted my fall but denied my shoulder surgery, calling it 'degenerative.' I'm still in a sling.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The fall itself happened when a beverage distributor's hand truck clipped the ladder I was on — their driver, their insurance, never pursued.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: A beverage distributor's hand truck clipped my ladder — their driver and insurance were never pursued.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the original mechanism involved an outside company
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Nobody clipped anything — the ladder shifted on our own wet floor; the fight is the surgery denial inside comp.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The ladder just shifted on our wet floor — the fight is the surgery denial.
scenarios.vetting_note: Buried third-party mechanism behind a treatment-denial complaint.
